name: Chicken Fried Steak Dupree
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds top round or chuck steak – cut into 2 to 3 inch thick pieces
2 cups saltine cracker crumbs – rolled fine
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix 1/2 cup flour, salt, and pepper together.
2. Pound the mixture onto both sides of the meat using the edge of a heavy plate or mallet.
3. Cut the meat into serving pieces.
4. Beat eggs with cream.
5. Heat oil in a heavy cast iron skillet over moderately high heat. Reserve 3 tablespoons flour.
6. Dredge steaks in remaining flour, dip in egg mixture, then cracker crumbs.
7. Place steaks in hot oil and brown well. Turn and brown other side.
8. Reduce heat to medium, cover skillet, cook for 15-20 minutes, turning occasionally until steaks are cooked through and tender.
9. Chicken fried steak should be well done but not dry. Remove steaks and drain on paper bags.
10. Add onion slices to pan and sauté quickly.
11. Pour off all but 3 tablespoons fat in skillet. Stir in reserved flour.
12. Incorporate any particles, cook for 1-2 minutes.
13. Stir in cream, then chicken broth. Season with Worcestershire and hot sauce.
14. Slice meat across grain and top with gravy.
Yield: 6 to 8 servings
